title: edithea guerrerensis
intro: Edithea guerrerensis, also known as Mexican valerian, is a flowering plant native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its sedative and anti-anxiety effects. The plant contains valerenic acid, which has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it a potential treatment for insomnia and restlessness. In herbal preparations, the root of the plant is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
